channel.title: The U-Curve Brain
channel.description: A macro-autonomous cognitive filter. Optimizing for agency, perspective, and resilience.

item.title: (Research) The Unexpected Mechanism By Which Confronting Death Deepens Human Connection
item.description:
Source: Reasons to be Cheerful

This piece explores the surprising phenomenon of how facing mortality can counter-intuitively serve as a catalyst for profound human connection and positive social change. It highlights specific instances where acknowledging life's fragility has prompted individuals to forge meaningful bonds and contribute to community resilience.

item.title: (Research) The Youngest Forbes 400 Member Vanished for 25 Years: What He Built in Secret
item.description:
Source: Farnam Street

Joe Liemandt, who dropped out of Stanford to become the youngest Forbes 400 member, then vanished from public life for 25 years, secretly built one of the most prolific software empires. This profile explores his quiet resurgence through ESW Capital and his current unexpected venture, Alpha School, hinting at a distinct vision for the future of education.

item.title: (Research) Half of All Species: How New Chinese Fossils Are Rewriting Earth's Early Life Story
item.description:
Source: Quanta Magazine

A remarkable discovery of exceptionally preserved Cambrian fossils in southern China is fundamentally altering scientific understanding of early life on Earth. This 'treasure trove' includes dozens of previously unknown species, providing an unprecedented detailed window into what life looked like at the end of the pivotal Cambrian explosion.

item.title: (Video) The Surprising Case for Why Your 'Self' Isn't Just an Illusion
item.description:
Source: Closer To Truth



Philosopher Rebecca Goldstein presents a compelling, counter-intuitive argument that the 'self' is not merely an illusion constructed by the mind, challenging prevalent views from Buddhism and Hume. She delves into the intricacies of consciousness and identity, suggesting that dismissing the self overlooks profound aspects of human experience and moral agency.

item.title: (Research) Jane Goodall's Archives Reveal the Surprising, Unglamorous Truth of Pioneering Field Science
item.description:
Source: The Leakey Foundation

Newly processed archives from Jane Goodall's early career reveal the surprising practical struggles behind her scientific breakthroughs, far removed from romanticized notions of fieldwork. The documents detail everything from hand-typed grant reports by candlelight to the challenge of balancing pioneering research with motherhood, offering a raw look at scientific dedication.

item.title: (Video) The Profound Cognitive Secret Peek-a-Boo Unlocks in Babies
item.description:
Source: TED-Ed



The seemingly simple game of peek-a-boo plays a crucial, unexpected role in a baby's cognitive development, specifically in understanding object permanence. By repeatedly 'disappearing' and 'reappearing,' parents help infants grasp that objects and people continue to exist even when out of sight, a foundational concept for later learning and social skills.

item.title: (Research) The Ancient Architectural Tradition That's Surprisingly Flourishing in the Modern World
item.description:
Source: Works in Progress RSS Feed

Despite most ancient architectural traditions having declined, Hindu temple architecture is unexpectedly experiencing a golden age of innovation and construction globally. This phenomenon suggests a unique resilience and adaptability within its traditions, allowing for continued relevance and growth in contemporary society.

item.title: (Video) Escher's most mind-bending piece
item.description:
Source: 3Blue1Brown



On "The Print Gallery", by M.C. Escher Full video: https://youtu.be/ldxFjLJ3rVY.

item.title: (Video) The Surprising Brain Mechanism Explaining Psychedelics' Therapeutic Power
item.description:
Source: Dr Ben Miles



Recent neuroscience has begun to unravel the complex brain mechanisms behind psychedelics' therapeutic effects, moving beyond mere anecdotal accounts. This includes insights into how these compounds temporarily disrupt habitual thought patterns and enhance neural plasticity, potentially offering new avenues for mental health treatment.

item.title: (Video) Britain's 'Modern Slavery' Fight: Who Actually Benefits When the UK Intervenes?
item.description:
Source: LSE



This LSE discussion critically examines how Britain's current anti-modern slavery efforts might be less about helping victims and more about national self-image or reinforcing existing power structures. It argues that these contemporary interventions can inadvertently obscure the UK's historical role in slavery and global inequality, rather than truly addressing its root causes.

item.title: Uncovering Reality's Hidden, Surprising Operating Principles
item.description: These items reveal a constant process of discovering hidden, often counter-intuitive principles that govern reality, perpetually re-shaping our understanding.

item.title: (Adage) Look before you leap (Consider outcomes to make calmer, smarter choices.)
(Reminder) True growth in your self, your bonds, or your calling is built with patient intention, not undone by the rapid currents of external fear.

(Today) Even as familiar certainties fray and the world's persistent problems demand our attention, there’s an expansive energy in uncovering the unseen forces and surprising truths that genuinely shape our reality.
item.description:
Even as familiar certainties fray and the world's persistent problems demand our attention, there’s an expansive energy in uncovering the unseen forces and surprising truths that genuinely shape our reality. Today’s collection invites you to explore the subtle mechanisms underlying consciousness itself, from ancient architectural wisdom to the profound human connections forged in the shadow of mortality. These narratives subtly challenge the easy assumptions we make about progress, history, or even our own identity, revealing a more intricate, dynamic story. This isn't about ignoring the difficult questions, Max, but about equipping yourself with the wider lens needed to actively build a future where understanding truly expands our world, rather than shrinking it.

How did today's sources land?
Excellent sources  |  Mixed  |  Weak sources today
